entry.title: Automate updates to my dashboard imported from an excel
entry.content:
Hi

You could help me with the following doubt, in the company we have a calculation sheet (excel), the spreadsheet is updated constantly by the Purchasing Team, the spreadsheet was uploaded to the SAC and a panel was created from it, therefore every time the spreadsheet is updated by the Purchasing Team I must access the SAC and re-import the form so that the panel in the SAC is updated, for the moment the work was very manual, Would you have any idea how I could automate? please.

Thank you, my best regards

entry.title: Strict mode in RAP BO behavior definition
entry.content:
Hi,

We read from the documentation that adding the strict mode ensures that a RAP business object is upgrade-safe. I know it is best practice to use strict ( 2 ), but there are times where we need to use strict ( 1 ) and we are just wondering whether if strict ( 1 ) is considered lifecycle stable and upgrade-safe?

entry.title: Empowering a Greener Future: SAP Utilities' Newsletter Highlights Innovation & Sustainability
entry.content:
Dear Utilities Community,

The latest edition of the SAP Utilities Newsletter offers a moment to reflect on the remarkable progress and achievements in the utilities industry. This year has been a year of change, marked by significant progress in the areas of customer satisfaction, sustainability, and technological innovation.

 

Key topics 

Use of AI and Cloud Technologies
Integrating artificial intelligence into utility company processes has been a decisive step forward. AI-powered solutions have optimized operations, improved decision-making, and enhanced service delivery. This issue delves into how these technologies are transforming the industry.

Celebrating Partnerships and Customer Success Stories
The newsletter also recognizes the invaluable role of SAP's customers and partners. These collaborations have driven innovation and created tailored solutions to meet the specific needs of the utilities sector. Success stories from companies such as EdERSA, Christchurch City Council, and IWB highlight the tangible benefits - from operational improvements to increased customer satisfaction.

Commitment to Sustainability
Sustainability remains a top priority for SAP. This year, SAP introduced solutions to reduce CO2 emissions and promote renewable energy. These efforts go beyond compliance, paving the way for a greener future. The newsletter highlights various projects and partnerships that have contributed to these goals, showcasing SAP's dedication to environmental protection.

Looking to the Future
Looking ahead to 2025, the SAP Utilities team is looking forward to the upcoming international SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference in Rotterdam. This event promises to be a center of knowledge sharing and networking, setting the stage for another year of innovation and growth.

In summary, the SAP Utilities Newsletter 2024 reflects the progress and potential of the utilities sector. It reflects a year of hard work, collaboration, and forward-thinking that has set the course for a better and more sustainable future. If you haven't received the newsletter, please read it online.

We look forward to providing you with news about SAP and the utilities industry again next year. If you are not yet registered, you can sign up here.

Happy Holidays and a wonderful New Year!

Your SAP Utilities Team

entry.title: Re: Async onInit - Route not trigerred
entry.content:
Had the same issue and resolved it likes this. Dont know if this is helpful for you:

static async fireEventAsync(eventName: string, eventParameters: object, component: Component): Promise<FireEventReturnType> {
	let aEventListeners,
		event,
		promises = [];

	// @TS-ignore
	const eventPool = new ObjectPool(Event);

	aEventListeners = (component as any).mEventRegistry[eventName];

	if (Array.isArray(aEventListeners)) {
		// Avoid issues with 'concurrent modification' (e.g. if an event listener unregisters itself).
		aEventListeners = aEventListeners.slice();
		event = eventPool.borrowObject(eventName, component, eventParameters); // borrow event lazily

		for (let oInfo of aEventListeners) {
			try {
				// Assuming each handler returns a promise
				promises.push(oInfo.fFunction.call(null, event));
			} catch (error) {
				Log.error("Error in event handler:", error as Error);
			}
		}
	}

	// Wait for all promises (i.e., async handlers) to resolve
	await Promise.all(promises);

	return {
		bPreventDefault: (event as any)?.bPreventDefault,
		mParameters: (event as any)?.mParameters,
		returnValue: promises[0]
	};
}

And usage of this method

const asyncEventPreFileProcessing = await Util.fireEventAsync("preFileProcessing", { file: file }, this.component);
			const isDefaultPrevented = asyncEventPreFileProcessing.bPreventDefault;

And in final code like:

// Event to check before uploading to app
this.spreadsheetUpload.attachCheckBeforeRead(async function (event) {
    return new Promise(async (resolve, reject) => {
        // Example
        console.log("Start async wait");
        await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 5000));
        console.log("End async wait");
        // Don't forget to resolve the promise
        resolve();
    });
}, this);

It is used in my Spreadsheet Importer Component:

Method fireEventAsync Implementation

Usage of this method

Sample usage in Code Documentation

entry.title: ABAP dump when calling odata v4 service using local proxy from a fiori app based on RAP BO
entry.content:
Hi,

I am getting this error whenever I call an odata service from a RAP BO using remote/local proxy.

This error is not there when I call the same code via a console app in ADT.

      DATA: lt_product     TYPE CL_API_PRODUCT_MPC=>TT_A_PRODUCTTYPE,
            lo_client_proxy  TYPE REF TO /iwbep/if_cp_client_proxy,
            lo_read_request  TYPE REF TO /iwbep/if_cp_request_read_list,
            lo_read_response TYPE REF TO /iwbep/if_cp_response_read_lst.

    DATA lv_relative_service_root TYPE string.



    TRY.

        lo_client_proxy = /iwbep/cl_cp_client_proxy_fact=>create_v4_local_proxy( VALUE #( service_id = 'API_PRODUCT' service_version = '0001' repository_id = 'SRVD_A2X' ) ).

        lo_read_request = lo_client_proxy->create_resource_for_entity_set( 'PRODUCT' )->create_request_for_read( ).
        lo_read_request->set_top( iv_top = 5 ).
        lo_read_response = lo_read_request->execute( ).

        "Retrieve the business data
        lo_read_response->get_business_data( IMPORTING et_business_data = lt_product ).

        LOOP AT lt_product INTO DATA(ls_product).
*          out->write( ls_product ).
        ENDLOOP.

      CATCH /iwbep/cx_cp_remote INTO DATA(lx_cp_remote).
        " Error handling
        DATA(lv_text1) = lx_cp_remote->get_longtext( ).
      CATCH /iwbep/cx_gateway INTO DATA(lx_gateway).
        " Error Handling
        DATA(lv_text2) = lx_gateway->get_longtext( ).
    ENDTRY.

  I didn't get the error when calling an odata v2 service using local proxy.

	alert("UI5 is ready");

entry.title: SAP Utilities Roadmap 2025 Onwards
entry.content:
The utilities industry will continue to play a key role in SAP's strategy and development topics. It is therefore important to us to provide customers, partners, and the entire SAP Utilities ecosystem with information about the planned innovations.

This year we have once again created a matrix of the respective industry topics and the corresponding innovations. This matrix represents an excerpt of the most important innovations and does not claim to be complete. Over time, further innovations may be added, and delivery periods may also change.

After an overview of the highlights, we will list the details for each topic; behind almost all innovations you will find a link to the SAP Roadmap Explorer with further details.

 

SAP Asset Performance Management

  • AI-powered Joule digital assistant for navigational and transactional use cases Link
  • AI-enabled visual inspection for asset condition monitoring (general availability) Link
  • Advanced asset health monitoring scenarios with embedded IoT Link
  • Custom AI models and algorithms Link

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

  • Utilizing artificial intelligence to streamline creation and planning of maintenance orders Link
  • Enable external collaboration with subcontractors and service providers Link
  • Enabling integration of service with advanced execution orders (billable maintenance) Link
  • Supporting linear fields in Find Maintenance Order and Operation Link (and many more roadmap items supporting linear assets)
  • Isolation management in a permit to work (lockout tagout) Link

SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration

  • Mass import export APIs for main technical objects Link
  • Digital nameplate Link
  • Enablement of digital twin handover with procurement Link

SAP Field Service Management

  • AI-assisted browsing of knowledge bases Link
  • Multiple technicians in auto-scheduling – crews Link
  • Fully automated planning on the service map Link

SAP Service and Asset Manager

  • Field service crew support Link
  • Management of non-stock items Link
  • OCR for quick data capture Link

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

  • Automatic creation of maintenance notifications for condition-enabled measuring points Link
  • Enable in-house refurbishment Link
  • OCR for quick data capture Link

SAP Maintenance Assistant mobile app

  • AI-enabled maintenance and service execution Link
  • Support for Esri maps integration Link
  • Supporting SAP Dynamic Forms Link

 

Metering

  • Graphical display of utilities interval data Link
  • More flexible status handling for energy data management formulas Link
  • Archiving of technical master data that is specific to the utilities industry Link
  • Enabling custom fields and logic for point-of-delivery and BPEM case data Link

Analytics

  • Extraction of Energy data management related master data Link
  • Using SAP Datasphere in the utilities industry – utilities sales contracts Link
  • Native sales statistics and unbilled revenue reporting in SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud Link

Convergent Invoicing

  • End-to-end reconciliation from event to GL in contract accounting and invoicing Link
  • Increase automation of invoicing processes by leveraging machine learning and predictive models Link
  • Reduce time and costs associated with simulation of invoice forecasts for subscription or usage alterations Link

Financials (FI-CA)

  • Display of utilities invoices Link and budget billing plans Link in a business partner financial overview
  • Budget billing plans in the Business Partner Financial Overview app Link
  • Display of utilities invoices in a business partner financial overview Link

 

Customer and Service Management

  • Generative AI–driven digital agent for customer self-service and customer engagement Link
  • Service management for utilities – round-off Link
  • Period control for life cycle events Link
  • Streamlining contract migration and bulk data processing Link
  • Extending pricing capabilities Link
  • Digital payments for service transactions  Link
  • Incorporating the Joule assistant Link

Sales for Commodity & Non-commodity

  • Alignment with the product model of SAP S/4HANA Link
  • Product replication using SAP Master Data Integration Link
  • Intelligent sales execution – target planning Link
  • Intelligent sales execution – product- and volume-based pipeline management Link
  • AI product configuration Link
  • New user interface for the document generation process on the Quote Link
  • Usage-based pricing with integration with SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management Link

Commerce Telco & Utilities Accelerator

  • Streamlined material replication for smoother integration Link

Marketing

  • Personalization and segmentation accelerator for utilities Link
  • Advanced personalization and segmentation accelerator for utilities Link

CX AI Toolkit

  • Shopping assistant agent: personalized recommendations Link

 

Distributed Energy Resources (DER)

  • Cancellation of measurement concept instances before device installation Link
  • Analytics for Long-Running Processes Link
  • Enabling validation checks of instances through an API Link
  • Export of a network graph for usage in third-party systems Link
  • Advanced asset health monitoring scenarios with embedded IoT Link
  • AI-powered Joule digital assistant for navigational and transactional use cases Link
  • Integrating AI-based electric vehicle supply equipment (EVSE) asset management Link
  • E-Mobility integration to complementary ecosystem solutions such as energy management Link

Circular Economy

  • Generate a generic waste transportation document to enable shipment of waste Link
  • Integration of waste management and purchasing processes Link
  • Initial Release of SAP Spend Control Tower to provide an enterprise view of total spend Link

Climate Action

  • Carbon emissions allowance management Link
  • Environment management for energy consumption data Link
  • Carbon border adjustment mechanism (CBAM): content with an operator focus Link

Sustainability Accounting

  • Collection and allocation of greenhouse gas emissions in a carbon journal Link
  • Importing emission quantities through file upload Link
  • Accounting for carbon allowances in a carbon journal Link
  • Enabling automatic carbon collections and automated allocations Link
  • Posting and analysis of carbon quantities on external suppliers / affiliated companies Link

E-mobility

  • Charging cost reimbursement for employee charging Link
  • Enabling import and processing of charging sessions from external charge point management systems Link
  • Smart Charging configuration based on user defined parameters Link
  • CO2-based electric vehicle (EV) charging and pricing tariffs handling Link
  • Compatibility with ISO 15118 to support plug-and-charge functionality Link
  • EV Driver authorizations and invoicing in charging groups Link
  • Unidirectional smart charging (V1G) and bi-directional smart charging (V2G) Link

Social Responsibility

  • Electronic submission of recordable injury or illness cases to OSHA as per the legal requirements (cloud) Link
  • Operational risk management: approval of safety instructions for chemicals and equipment (cloud) Link
  • Support for standard, analytical content for workplace safety and social responsibility (cloud) Link
  • Operational risk assessment: visualization of risk history Link

Dangerous Goods Management and Recycling Administration

  • Storing attributes of batteries as basis for the dangerous goods classification Link
  • Additional regulation-specific details for dangerous goods regulations Link
  • Defining customer-specific dangerous goods description templates Link
  • Support for USG-04 in IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations Link

Holistic Steering and Reporting

  • Narrative disclosures, ESG-metric definition, and data collection Link
  • Custom metrics with specific dimension breakdown Link

Please consider that this information and SAP‘s strategy and possible future developments are subject to change and may be changed by SAP at any time for any reason without notice. This document is provided without a warranty of any kind, either express or implied, including but not limited to, the implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, or non-infringement.

entry.title: Projecting SAP PaPM Cloud artifacts in SAP Datasphere with the use of the Command Line Interface
entry.content:
Friendly Reminder:

All users, particularly those with technically inclined with Command Line Interface can follow this blog post.

Flexibility of SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud (SAP PaPM CLD) has been extended even more with the all-new BYOD feature where you can use your existing Standalone SAP HANA Cloud Database  (Standalone HCDB) or Datasphere HANA Cloud Runtime Database (DSP HCDB) to be the database of your SAP PaPM CLD subscription.

Exposure of  database to SAP Datasphere using your runtime database is already achievable via Smart Data Access(SDA) and Smart Data Integration (SDI).

To know more about this integration, see: Integrating SAP PaPM Cloud & SAP Datasphere (DWC) and StepByStep SAP PaPM Cloud <> SAP PaPM On-Premise Integration.

Meanwhile, if you have a subscription of SAP PaPM Cloud with your own Database (BYOD) using your existing DSP HCDB then you do not have to create a connection anymore in your SAP Datasphere as your existing space is your Database already.

With this BYOD setup alone, you can already create a graphical view manually from the exposed generated artifacts in the space where your Standard and Universal Model HDIs are mapped.

In addition to the flexibility given by the BYOD setup, you can also use and access the feature of SAP Datasphere via Command Line Interface. See, Accessing SAP Datasphere via the Command Line.

By simply knowing the name of the artifacts from SAP PaPM Cloud and running simple command, this Command Line Interface can help us to recreate a local table automatically in SAP Datasphere.

You can even save the commands in your local and reuse it by only changing the name of the artifacts based on your requirements. This approach can save time and reducing the manual effort on the creation via UI.

To achieve this integration, we will be needing to download NodeJS which I will show to you later on the procedure and sap-datasphere-exhibitor.

But before we proceed, what is sap-datasphere-exhibitor? sap-datasphere-exhibitor is designed for exposing remote schema objects into SAP Datasphere repository and remotely create SAP Datasphere objects from specific artifact in SAP PaPM Cloud via Command Line Interface. For more information, see https://www.npmjs.com/package/sap-datasphere-exhibitor  . 

Now, let me show to you how to achieve this integration setup.

Prerequisites:

  1. You have an existing subscription to SAP PaPM Cloud Standard OR Universal Model using BYOD subscription that uses the DSP HCDB. More information: Subscription.
  2. You have an environment on SAP PaPM Cloud Standard OR Universal Model that uses a processing function.

Procedure:

Installation of NodeJS and Authentication to SAP Datasphere

  1. Go to How to Install Node.js for the installation of Node JS,
  1. Choose Prebuilt Installer
  2. Select the latest version and the OS you are using in your computer. (On this example I have the version v21.7.3
  3. Choose Download Node js

    2.  Open the Node JS command prompt.

    3.  Install the SAP Datasphere CLI by typing “npm install -g @SAP/datasphere-cli” then press enter.

    4. Create OAuth Client for the usage of SAP Datasphere Command Line Interface, OAuth Client purpose must be Interactive usage. For more information, see Create OAuth 2.0 Clients to Authenticate Against SAP Datasphere.

   5. In the Command Line Interface, use the OAuth Client created to login to Datasphere by typing “Datasphere login”. More information: OAuth Interactive Usage

SAP PaPM Cloud – Generating Artifacts

As mentioned above, Standard and Universal Model can generate an artifact from activated processing functions. The input of these processing functions can be from a Local Model Tables / Entities or coming from an external source like SAP Analytics Cloud(SAC).

Now, I will show to you how to get these generated artifacts that will be replicated to SAP Datasphere via Command Line Interface.

Let me show to you first how it is done in Universal Model.

Standard Model will be added soon.

Universal Model

Modeling Setup

  1. In Universal Model, I have created a SAC Model connection to consume the existing Model I have from SAC together with the reader function that will fetch its data records to Universal Model.
  2. These data records consumed will serve as an Input to our Calculation function that contains a simple calculation.
  3. Activation of the Environment is necessary so the artifacts from our function will be generated to the underlying database of your SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model.
  4. When everything is setup, you may now proceed on running this function to calculate the data records from the Input function. With this, the calculated data records will be available in the Show data screen of our Calculation function.

Getting the Artifacts from Universal Model

  1. Open the SAP HANA Cloud Database Explorer of the space you have used as the database of your SAP PaPM Cloud subscription and login as the _RT user. These credentials can be seen from SAP PaPM Cloud SM > Hamburger Menu > Settings.
  2. Follow the steps below to open the available the Views section where the generated artifacts are saved.
  1. Expand the Catalog folder
  2. Choose Views
  3. Search the name of the Artifacts with this format (Z_EXT_(ENVID)_(FID)).

    Note: The schema where the artifacts will be saved are depending on the HDI Container you have created from your Own Database subscription with SAP PaPM Cloud.

      3.  You can also confirm the content of this artifacts by following the instruction below:

  1. Choose the searched artifact.
  2. Choose Open Data.

    Now that we have identified the generated artifact from our processing function of Universal Model, let’s now go back to the Command Line Interface to communicate with SAP Datasphere.

Installation and running of Datasphere-exhibitor

  1. Create Database Analysis User in SAP Datasphere.
  2. Install the Datasphere-exhibitor by typing “npm i sap-datasphere-exhibitor”  in the Command Line Interface.
  3. Prepare the configuration JSON file with SAP HANA Client connection options and put Database Analysis user connection details into it. Example:

 

 

 

{
    "host": "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000.hana.prod-xx00.hanacloud.ondemand.com",
    "port": "443",
    "uid": "DWCDBUSER#TEST",
    "pwd": "Password123"
}​

 

 

 

      4. Save it as a file, eg: options.json where you have installed the sap-Datasphere-exhibitor.
      5. Run Datasphere-exhibitor command for the remote creation of table of artifact to your SAP PaPM Cloud Datasphere by following this format:

datasphere-exhibitor remote-view-to-local-table --space SPACE_ID --schema CONTAINER_NAME --connection-file ./options.json --artifact XYZ
datasphere-exhibitor remote-view-to-fact-view --space SPACE_ID --schema CONTAINER_NAME --connection-file ./options.json --artifact XYZ --measures FIELD:SUM

With the successful RUN of the Datasphere-exhibitor command above, it will show the details of the artifact accordingly:

It also might happen that your requirements in your environment will get bigger and you will be needing a new artifact from your environment.

No worries with that as you can just reuse the command you have performed from previous steps and changing the artifact accordingly.

After following the procedure above, you may now go to your SAP Datasphere and proceed on configuring the Local table created from our generated artifact in SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model.

You may also create a View on top of this object created for this to be exposed on SAP Analytics Cloud.

For more information, Consume SAP Datasphere OData in SAP Analytics Cloud via an OData Service .

Once the view is exposed, you may use this consumed data from SAP Datasphere to in the analytical reporting tools of SAP Analytics Cloud.

That’s all, I hope you learned another integration that you can use in your analytical workflow from using the SAP PaPM Cloud together with sap-datasphere-exhibitor.

See you again on the next integration topic!

entry.title: Re: US Form I-9 Concepts for SuccessFactors Onboarding 2.0
entry.content:
This is a great article and very informative.  I do have a quick question related to re-verification in the system. We have just transitioned to SuccessFactors in the last year and we have re-verifications popping up for existing employees that were not processed through E-Verify in SuccessFactors.  Is there a way to trigger the re-verification process in that scenario?  I tried the launching via the trigger action, but was unsure of what to provide in the "process" field.  I tried the employee id, but that didn't work, and my assumption was that it wants the original I9 process # which doesn't exist.  Just wondered if there was a way to transition them into the system for this and future re-verifications. 

Best regards, 

Stephanie Bird

entry.title: SAC Live models slow performance in story
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud 

Hi I am new to SAC and SAP community. I notice that all stories connected to live models are very slow.

I am curious is it possible to optimize this? I get that they reload the first time new data but every time I press something it will start reloading again. Would it be possible to only reload it once automatically and after that only if the viewer wants to have their data up to date to manually refresh it? And if this is not possible and a acquired model is recommended, why would you even use a live connection?

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Integration: HTTP vs. OData Receiver Adapter
entry.content: There is another important disadvantage worth mentioning: there is no Throw Exception On Failure option as in the HTTP adapter, which forces us to handle the exception in a specific way and add more logic in the iFlow.

entry.title: A Small Change for a Greener Tomorrow
entry.content: What’s one habit you’ve embraced to live more sustainably? Whether it’s using reusable bags, reducing food waste, or walking instead of driving, every small step counts. For example: I have started growing trees in the piece of land I own in India.  My land is small but then thought it would be an humble start towards reforestation.  It is painful to see only about small percentage of the trees I planted grew up to 6ft tall. Cheers.

entry.title: Re: pcr payroll
entry.content:
Additional information:

At the beginning of a Schema, it is wise to add a PCR that will clear out all temporary variables that will be created in that schema, specially if it could make a loop, using Operation ZERO=&XXXX followed by ZERO=&YYYY, ZERO=&ZZZZ and so on)

Using a Constant (from V_T511K) instead of using a number enables you to change the value over time without having to delimit your PCR and create a new node in it.  Maybe in a year or three the comparison will not be with 50,000 but with 45,000 or 60,000.  It's easier to change the value of a constant than to change a PCR.

entry.title: SAP Cloud Foundry - Python and Cloud Connector - HTTP
entry.content:
SAP Cloud Foundry enables developers to create full-stack applications with seamless backend integration to on-premise systems. This blog explains how to use Python to connect to on-premise resources through the SAP Cloud Connector. Python is the preferred language in the AI ecosystem, making it increasingly important for developing intelligent agents. For instance, we may want our agent to access APIs in an S4HANA system. With the Cloud Connector, this can be done securely within the enterprise environment.

Access HTTP-based resources:

In this scenario, we want to connect to an HTTP-based API, such as a RESTful API or an OData service, using Python in SAP Cloud Foundry. To do this, we need to set up the SAP Cloud Connector.

For this demonstration, we will use a simple "Hello World" server running locally on my computer. The goal is to access this local server from the SAP Cloud Foundry runtime through the Cloud Connector. This setup will show how to bridge cloud applications with on-premise resources.

Szenario Architecture

Prerequisite:

Before proceeding with the steps outlined in this guide, it is essential to have an instance of the SAP Cloud Connector installed. While it is possible to install the cloud connector on a server, for the purposes of this demonstration, we will be using a Windows machine. We recommend following the instructions provided in this blog (https://blogs.sap.com/2021/09/05/installation-and-configuration-of-sap-cloud-connector/) to install and configure the cloud connector.

Second requirement is a BTP subaccount with a Cloud Foundry Environment. To this subaccount we will connect the Cloud Connector.

1. Cloud Connector Configuration:


The first step is to create a configuration in the Cloud Connector that connects to our subaccount and exposes the HTTP resource. For the purpose of this demonstration, I ran a small Node.js server on my Windows machine that outputs "Hello World".

Localhost Example Server


To create the configuration, navigate to the admin interface for the cloud connector and create a "Cloud to On-Premise" configuration.

Cloud To On-Premise Configuration


In the screenshot above, you can see that I exposed the internal host "localhost" with port 3333 via a virtual host called "virtualhost". This virtual host is the host that we will be requesting from the BTP side. For the time being, I exposed all paths using an unrestricted access policy, but in production scenarios, access policies can be defined more granularly.

BTP Registered Cloud Connectors


On the BTP end, we can check the cockpit and the connected cloud connectors in the respective menu tab. If you cannot see this tab, you may be missing some roles. It is important to note that we see the LocationID "FELIXLAPTOP", which is an identifier that distinguishes multiple cloud connectors connected to the same subaccount.

2. Create Connectivity Service instance:

To use the Cloud Connector from our runtime environment, we first need a connectivity service instance. Here’s how to do it:

In the BTP Cockpit, go to your desired Subaccount and create a new service instance, as shown below.

We use the "Connectivity Service" with the plan "lite". Give the service instance a name, assign it to a space, and click create.

The important credentials are stored in a service key. To get these credentials, we create a service key.

 

{
    "clientid": "sb-sampleclientid!b3008|connectivity!b137",
    "clientsecret": "****-****-****-****",
    "url": "https://tenant_name.authentication.sap.hana.ondemand.com",
    "identityzone": "sample-zone",
    "tenantid": "xxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx",
    "tenantmode": "dedicated",
    "verificationkey": "-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----\nXXXXXX...\n-----END PUBLIC KEY-----",
    "xsappname": "sampleappname!b3008|connectivity!b137",
    "uaadomain": "authentication.sap.hana.ondemand.com",
    "credential-type": "binding-secret",
    "onpremise_proxy_host": "connectivityproxy.internal.cf.sap.hana.ondemand.com",
    "onpremise_proxy_http_port": "20003",
    "onpremise_proxy_ldap_port": "20001",
    "onpremise_proxy_port": "20003",
    "onpremise_proxy_rfc_port": "20001",
    "onpremise_socks5_proxy_port": "20004",
    "token_service_domain": "authentication.sap.hana.ondemand.com",
    "token_service_url": "https://tenant_name.authentication.sap.hana.ondemand.com"
}

 

We now have a set of credentials, as shown above. The key details are:

  1. OAuth Credentials: These include the "clientid", "clientsecret", and "url". They are used to authenticate when accessing the proxy.

  2. Proxy Details: These include the "onpremise_proxy_host" and the various ports, such as "onpremise_proxy_port", "onpremise_proxy_http_port", and "onpremise_socks5_proxy_port". These details are configured as the proxy to route traffic. The proxy securely tunnels requests through the Cloud Connector to the on-premise destination.

3. Developing a Cloud Foundry App:

 

Now let’s put this together in a simple Python example to perform an actual HTTP request:

The Python script will send a request using Python's standard requests library. We use the connection details provided by the Connectivity Service. To route the traffic through the Cloud Connector, we specify the proxy configuration, including the host, port, and the proxy-authentication header.

 

import requests

# Connectivity Service Service Key
connectivity_service_key = {
    "clientid": "sb-sampleclientid!b3008|connectivity!b137",
    "clientsecret": "****-****-****-****",
    "url": "https://tenant_name.authentication.sap.hana.ondemand.com",
    "identityzone": "sample-zone",
    "tenantid": "xxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx",
    "tenantmode": "dedicated",
    "verificationkey": "-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----\nXXXXXX...\n-----END PUBLIC KEY-----",
    "xsappname": "sampleappname!b3008|connectivity!b137",
    "uaadomain": "authentication.sap.hana.ondemand.com",
    "credential-type": "binding-secret",
    "onpremise_proxy_host": "connectivityproxy.internal.cf.sap.hana.ondemand.com",
    "onpremise_proxy_http_port": "20003",
    "onpremise_proxy_ldap_port": "20001",
    "onpremise_proxy_port": "20003",
    "onpremise_proxy_rfc_port": "20001",
    "onpremise_socks5_proxy_port": "20004",
    "token_service_domain": "authentication.sap.hana.ondemand.com",
    "token_service_url": "https://tenant_name.authentication.sap.hana.ondemand.com"
}


# Target Application Details (replace with your app information)
application_host = "virtualhost"
application_port = 3333
application_path = "/hello"
location_id = "FELIXLAPTOP"  # Adjust to match your Cloud Connector location

def get_connectivity_service_token(client_id, client_secret, token_service_url):
    """
    Fetches an OAuth token from the SAP Connectivity Service.
    """
    response = requests.post(
        url=token_service_url,
        params={"grant_type": "client_credentials"},
        auth=(client_id, client_secret)
    )

    if response.status_code != 200:
        print(f"Error: {response.status_code} - {response.text}")
        exit(-1)

    return response.json().get("access_token")


def example_http_request(host, port, path, auth_token, location_id, proxy_host):
    """
    Performs an HTTP GET request through SAP Cloud Connector.
    """
    url = f"http://{host}:{port}{path}"
    headers = {
        "Proxy-Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}",
        "SAP-Connectivity-SCC-Location_ID": location_id
    }
    proxies = {"http": proxy_host}

    response = requests.get(url, headers=headers, proxies=proxies, verify=False)
    return response


# Fetch OAuth Token
print("Fetching OAuth token...")
token = get_connectivity_service_token(
    connectivity_service_key["clientid"],
    connectivity_service_key["clientsecret"],
    connectivity_service_key["token_service_url"] + "/oauth/token"
)
print("Token acquired successfully.")

# Build proxy URL
proxy_host = "http://" + connectivity_service_key["onpremise_proxy_host"] + ":" + connectivity_service_key["onpremise_proxy_port"]

# Perform HTTP Request
print("Sending HTTP request through SAP Cloud Connector...")
response = example_http_request(application_host, application_port, application_path, token, location_id, proxy_host)
print("Response received:")
print(response.content.decode("utf-8"))
print("Script execution completed.")

 

 

First, we obtain a token from the OAuth 2.0 Token Service URL. This is done using the get_connectivity_service_token function, where we send the necessary credentials.

Note: Make sure to add /oauth/token at the end of the Token Service URL.

The response will return a Bearer Token, which we need to perform the actual HTTP request.

In the example_http_request function, we use Python's requests library to send the request. The proxies option allows us to route the traffic through the Cloud Connector's HTTP proxy. For this, we:

  • Combine the proxy_host (connectivity service proxy host) and port (e.g., HTTP port).
  • For other traffic types, such as TCP-based traffic, we would use the SOCKS5 port.

We also include important headers:

  • Proxy-Authorization: This includes the Bearer Token ("Bearer <token>").
  • SAP-Connectivity-Location-ID: This optional header specifies the location ID of the connected Cloud Connector. For example, if my Cloud Connector is connected under "FELIXLAPTOP", I include this value.

The actual destination URL points to the virtual host and virtual port configured in the Cloud Connector. For this example:

  • virtualhost:3333 communicates with my local server.
  • The path /hello returns a sample message.

Finally, we combine all steps into a simple sequential script to demonstrate the entire flow.

To make it deployable on Cloud Foundry, we add a standard manifest.yaml file.

 

---
applications:
- name: cloud_connector_test_task
  memory: 128MB
  buildpack: python_buildpack
  command: python app.py

 

It sets the buildpack to python_buildpack.

Additionally, we include a requirements.txt file in the directory.

 

requests

 

 

Ending up with the following structure:

 

my-python-cloud-connector-app/
│
├── manifest.yaml
├── requirements.txt
├── app.py (or your main Python script)

 

Note: The script we created obviously does not run on our local environment. Because a) we cannot connect to the connectivity service from outside of the BTP and b) the destination URL is not reachable from our local environment. In this blog, I show how to use a hybrid testing setup to develop against backend resources that  are behind the cloud connector.

4. Testing the Application:

Now lets deploy using the following command:

 

cf push cloud_connector_test_task --task

 

You push your script as a task (one-time executable) to the Cloud Foundry space. Then, you start the task using the following command:

 

cf run-task cloud_connector_test_task --command "python app.py" --name example_task
cf logs cloud_connector_test_task --recent

 

 

This will show the logs from the latest task execution. It may take a few seconds for the task to start and complete.

In the logs, if everything worked correctly, you should see the "Hello World" message from your on-premises PC, confirming that the connection through the Cloud Connector was successful.

 

2024-12-17T15:25:11.52+0100 [APP/TASK/example_task/0] OUT Fetching OAuth token...
2024-12-17T15:25:11.69+0100 [APP/TASK/example_task/0] OUT Token acquired successfully.
2024-12-17T15:25:11.69+0100 [APP/TASK/example_task/0] OUT Sending HTTP request through SAP Cloud Connector...
2024-12-17T15:25:13.66+0100 [APP/TASK/example_task/0] OUT Response received:
2024-12-17T15:25:13.66+0100 [APP/TASK/example_task/0] OUT {"message":"Hello World 2024-12-17T14:25:13.444Z"}
2024-12-17T15:25:13.66+0100 [APP/TASK/example_task/0] OUT Script execution completed.

 


That means we have successfully established connectivity via the cloud connector and made a HTTP request.

In the next blog I will show how to create a TCP socket in Python to connect to TCP resources.

Hope you find the content of this blog helpful. Feel free to comment for further clarifications.

entry.title: Re: Method overloading n overriding
entry.content:
As others stated, ABAP has no "real" overloading.

But we noticed there's some special case of overloading when the extended syntax check told us that >>Method "LOG" overwrites the predefined function "LOG"<<.

We were puzzled at first but as you see could figure it out.

See https://github.com/SAP/styleguides/blob/main/clean-abap/CleanABAP.md#avoid-obscuring-built-in-functions for another example.

entry.title: Re: A Small Change for a Greener Tomorrow
entry.content:
I reuse bags, don't waste food, walk a lot. I do that because it feels good, not because it counts. It does not count at all and there will not be a greener tomorrow. Don't fool ourselves - those small steps are by far outweighed by global players.

You can plant as many trees as you want, which is notable by itself, but at the same time many, many, many more trees are mowed down in tropical forests. We can reduce consumer plastic consumption, but that is just a drop in the ocean of oil drilled globally - drill, baby, drill.

entry.title: Group Valuation - 5W2
entry.content:
Hi, 

We'r in the beginning of an implementation project with SAP public cloud. Our customer plannes to include scope item for Group Valuation 5W2 in there system, they have also intercompany flows within there business. I've understood that Group Valuations needs to be activated during the initial scoping and can not be activated later on. However, I found SAP-note 3093830 - SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Release Restriction Note for Group Valuation and have some follow up questions.

The note says that there are some incompatible scope items if you plan to use 5W2 and from our DDA we've included 1HO & 1MX which has been meantioned in the note, so my question is how should we handle this? Is there any scope items that can be activated insted of 1HO and 1MX to make sure we dont have any incompatible scope items?

Kind Regards, Sanne

I am trying to add timestamp ZZTIMESTAMP field in BUT0BK table which will be displayed at UI level. Whenever user is creating/changing BP and he add/change Bank data then this custom attribute ZZTIMESTAMP will be updated by user.

I am unable to enhance standard structure for SMT mapping which is BUS_EI_BUPA_BANKDETAIL. 

There is similar post 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/error-in-smt-mapping-adding-field-in-bank-details-mdg-supplier/qaq-p/10720445

But there is no concrete solution. Any solution on this where we can enhance Bankdetail Entity at UI level using custom attribute.

We are using MDG co-deployment solution.

Thanks & Regards

Pratik Wankhade

entry.title: Re: Trouble getting extracted line items to show in an approval form
entry.content:
Hi @BunchChris ,

(I don't know if the issue still persists)

  1. Instead of creating individual list objects for description, quantity, unitPrice and amount, create one custom data type (lineItem) that contains these fields.
  2. Create a list of this custom data type (lineItem) before your loop.
  3. Create a new variable of this lineItem data type in the loop.
  4. Fill each field, i.e. description, quantity, etc.
  5. Add the lineItem to your list of line items
  6. If you pass this as output parameter it can be used as the list_line_item_fields that you map for your Workflow Form.

entry.title: Book freight to material/inventory without having a goods receipt
entry.content:
Hello everyone,


I would like to charge additional order costs such as freight to my material or the stock within an purchase order. I have seen that this usually works via conditions. Now I have the problem that the supplier should be paid in advance or independently of the goods receipt. However, as no goods receipt has yet been posted, I don't believe that a posting and payment can be made for the product due to the missing 3-way match. It can also happen that not only one but also several items are to be added.

How is it possible for me to assign freight costs to a specific material and still be able to pay the freight companies in advance?

 

Thanks a lot for your support.

 

BR

Henrik

entry.title: Integration Center - PMGM API - Competency Section Form Rating not available
entry.content:
Hi,

We have a challenge and would like to know if anyone encountered this type of requirement before or have suggestions on how to approach it, I’d greatly appreciate your input.

Challenge:
We need to fetch two overall ratings from a performance form via Integration Center—one from each of two competency sections: Competency Section 1 (A) and Competency Section 2 (B). Currently, we’re only able to fetch ratings from one section at a time and haven’t been able to retrieve both simultaneously.

Context:
The two ratings are required for importing into the Custom01 and Potential Trend portlets to be visible in the matrix report. Our goal is to build an integration that automates this data transfer to the standard portlets, eliminating the need for manual imports.

 

Performance Form “2025 Talent Review”

Section: Objective Competency Summary

Label: A & B Overview

A > Overall Rating 2.0 (example)

B > Overall Rating 2.2 (example)

 

 

 

Thank you,

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio Process insights to process intelligence

Hi @Manish_Srimathula ,

thanks for your request and sorry for the delay of our response!

As it seems like you have SAP Signavio Process Insights running (live and data in it) and SAP Signavio Process Intelligence licensed, you can ease your SAP Signavio Process Intelligence Go-Live by using our Plug and Gain approach. SAP Signavio Plug and Gain lets you combine the capabilities of SAP Signavio Process Insights and SAP Signavio Process Intelligence to reduce the time needed to thoroughly prepare your transformation from months to just hours, improve the deployment quality, and provide benefits to the business.

Here, you can find helpful resources to getting started with SAP Signavio Plug and Gain:

  • Blogpost - General Guide: Getting started with PnG: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/general-guide-getting-started-with-the-sap-signavio-plug-and-gain-approach/ba-p/13942139
  • Blogpost - Technical Guide: Getting started with PnG: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/technical-guide-getting-started-with-the-sap-signavio-plug-and-gain/ba-p/13934261
  • Plug and Gain Delivery Request Form: https://me.sap.com/notes/3529579
  • Notes PnG Value Accelerators Packages: https://me.sap.com/notes/3496399/E

In case, you want to jump on a call, feel free to shoot me a message via ascha.ahmed@sap.com 

Kind regards

Ascha (as part of the virtual Customer Success Team)

entry.title: Re: Depreciation start date is taking month beginning dat...
entry.content:
See period control in help https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/8865d153c9684608e10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

And dates section https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/9736dd51e2193946e10000000a44538d.html?locale=en-US

With LINS, which depreciates using 12 periods, this is expected.

entry.title: Join us at the Integration Modernization Summit for SAP Partners
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business environment, the need for organizations to have an agile and scalable integration architecture has become more critical than ever before. As technology continues to evolve, modernizing integration architecture becomes crucial for organizations to stay competitive. In the SAP ecosystem partners play an important role helping organizations to unlock the full potential of modern integration technologies SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) provides.

Hence, we would like to call all SAP partners to join us for the Integration Modernization Summit for SAP Partners to get valuable insights and updates about SAP's integration strategy, SAP’s integration portfolio, SAP’s best practices for integration and SAP’s offerings for partners. This event is scheduled for January 27th and 28th 2025 at SAP in St. Leon-Rot (Germany). The agenda of this event is designed to meet the needs of different SAP partner types such as sell, build and service partners.

Let’s take a look at the preliminary agenda of this summit:

Explore integration modernization and business opportunities with SAP Integration Suite

We plan to kick-off the first day in the early afternoon with lectures to set the stage for the entire event:

  • Integrate to innovate - SAP’s integration strategy
  • Bridging the gap - Exploring the untapped potential for integration
  • Uplevel your integration along your ERP transformation

In the afternoon we  plan to offer further sessions allowing you to learn from SAP experts about how sell, service and implement SAP Integration Suite for modernizing integration. We plan to close the day with an evening event which will offer a lot of networking opportunities in a relaxed atmosphere.

Enjoy deep dive into sessions on integration modernization

Let’s start the second day of the event with lectures about SAP partner program offerings, integration best practices and product innovations, such as:

  • Empowering Partner Success - programs & resources for SAP Partners
  • Defining and executing an integration strategy based on ISA-M using SAP Integration Suite together with SAP LeanIX enterprise architecture management solutions
  • Future innovations planned for SAP Integration Suite and SAP Datasphere

In the afternoon you will have the opportunity to join various breakout sessions to learn more about how to sell, transform and innovate with SAP Integration Suite. Before closing the event take the chance to meet the experts and to engage with your peers.

Check out the event page for further details and updates or simply register here (for SAP partners only).

Looking forward to seeing you all hopefully soon 😊

entry.title: Extracting data from screenshots (with and without AI)
entry.content:
We've probably all been there dozens of times:
Having humbly requested information from a co-worker in the unshakable faith one possesses in their colleague's thoughtfulness, envisioning the forthcoming data neatly packaged in copy-paste-ready text. Only to see that faith crumble into a thousand despairing fragments upon the realization that they have gallantly sent... a screenshot?! 😕

While one surely could ask the aforementioned colleague to export the data properly on his end and resend it, there is no telling when he/she might come back. It might even be the next day. Trying to evangelize people into ideally not sending screenshots in such cases is only worth trying if you collaborate with them on a regular basis.

Transcribing the screenshot yourself is (assuming any reasonable amount of data - let's say 10+ material numbers) not an option as its dull & time consuming work and - let's be honest - feels like admitting defeat for any remotely tech-savvy human being.

So, what other options are there?
I tried the ones that seemed most obvious and accessible to me - so you don't have to.  

 

Screen grabber (TechSmith Snagit):

Allows you to extract text from a subselection or from whole screenshot (which even preserves table structures) or a sub-area.
While this method is rather quick, the accuracy is not convincing, as it has a tendency to confuse "O", "0" and  "Q".

 

MS OneNote: Copy Text from Picture 

Also OneNote offers a rudimentary extract text functionality. Here you can only extract all text from a whole image. OneNote's "Copy Text from Picture" displays the same tendency to confuse "O", "0" and  "Q". Table structures are not preserved. Instead it seems to interpret table borders crossing as the letter "L". It also occasionally omits portions of material numbers:

 

Mobile phone standard apps (Android Gallery) 

Turns out, even the standard "Gallery" app on Android 13+ is capable of extracting text. After taping the "Extract Text" button ("T" - bottom right), it will highlight all text it recognizes, whereafter you can select the text and copy it to the clipboard. Accurately selecting multiple bits of text is quite tedious though. Also it confused "Q" an "O" in one case.

Being an android person, I could not try this so far but the standard "Photos" app on iOS 15+ should have a similar capability called "Live Text" (has to be enabled in setting). 

 

 Large Language Models (LLMs)

Most of the younger large language models are also capable of processing images as input data - so why not give them a spin as well? At least with the examples I used, I found describing in natural language what to extract once and not having to select/copy/paste n times to be generally more convenient. While all LLMs I tried were able to "understand" the task and correctly identified the information to extract, there were some differences in accuracy. I used SAP AI Launchpad to access all of the LLMs except for CoPilot.

Anthropic Claude-3-Opus

...displayed a similar tendency to confuse "Q", "O" and "0" as observed with other methods:

 

Anthropic Claude-3-Haiku

While accurately recognizing "Q", "O" and "0", Haiku tends to (though rarely) sneak in additional digits:

 

 

Anthropic Claude-3.5-sonnet / OpenAI GPT 4o / Google Gemini-1.5-flash / MS Copilot

All these models accurately recognized & returned the text from my sample screenshots:

As this was my first time trying MS Copilot I was naively expecting it to be more deeply integrated in Windows and capable of accessing files and mails but you have to copy/paste the screenshot in the prompt (or upload it) just like with any other LLM: 

 

TL;DR

First of all, keep in mind the sample size of this little experiment is rather small (<5 screenshots total) so you likely will stumble on different issues.
Still - based on that small sample: Assuming all alternatives are accessible, the younger LLMs seem to offer the best mix of ease of use and accuracy. However, even those are not perfect: For example all LLMs missed suffixes in material numbers containing several spaces.
While their use definitely saves time, the results still should be double checked. 

PS: If you use other methods, feel free to mention them in a comment.

entry.title: Re: Migration - BP - Partner Fuctions - multiple contacts
entry.content:
Hi @Tiago_Pereira,

Contact persons are created as separate business partners using internal numbering. Since this new numbering is only created during the migration process, you can't use the contact person number as contact ID for partner functions in a single step.

To assign a contact person as partner, first create the supplier with the contact person but without the purchasing data. Then, use the Supplier - extend existing record by new org levels migration object as a second step to assign the purchasing data together with the contact person in partner capacity.

I would recommend reviewing the migration object documentation: Supplier

Hope this helps.

Arturo Montes de Oca

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM Health Monitoring metric threshold values setup and best practices
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

We are configuring SAP Cloud ALM Health Monitoring. We are trying to decide the values for metric thresholds. What are the recommended best practice for the metric threshold limits for Alerting and notifications?

Thanks!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM Health Monitoring metric threshold valu...
entry.content:
This topic is addressed in detail in KBA 3553320 - How to decide metric threshold values for SAP Cloud ALM Health Monitoring? The KBA outlines some example scenarios using an imagined system landscape, and how their holistic information is used to make informed decisions when establishing metric threshold values.

In short, it is fine to use the pre-packaged "default" threshold values. But you will get maximum value out of Health Monitoring if you opt for custom values. To determine the Custom values, you must consider several factors in order to determine the importance of a metric, and thus what are appropriate threshold values. These factors can include:

  • The role the System+Client/Cloud Service plays in the landscape (Dev, Quality, etc.).
  • If the metric reaches "Critical", do you consider this a potential "business down" scenario? 
  • Do you need to be reactive at an earlier stage when a metric value starts to rise, or can you wait until much later for it to be addressed? 

Customers know their own landscape and business best, so they are equipped to outline these factors in great detail. Outlining these details for each System+Client/Cloud Service and metric allows you to holistically determine if a metric is important in the first place, and what values best represent the business needs.

SAP Support cannot provide bespoke detailed advice for Customers, as that is not a product issue/bug and instead a Consulting topic that must be addressed by an appropriate organization. If assistance is required, you will need to leverage a Consulting service to get further input.

entry.title: Re: Replacement for CO06 in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?
entry.content:
Hello @t_jungesblut 

In the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, backorder processing follows a different approach compared to the On-Premise system, where the CO06 transaction code is typically used to execute this process

Here are the steps for backorder processing

  1. Configure BOP Segment : Go to the Configure BOP Segment Fiori application and create two segments: one for "Win" and another for "Lose," applying the appropriate conditions. This setup allows you to reallocate the confirmed quantity from lower-priority customers sales orders to higher-priority customers sales orders.

     

     

  2. Configure BOP Variant : Go to the Configure BOP Variant Fiori application and create a variant as shown below. After creating the variant, click the Simulate button to validate it. If the simulation completes successfully, you can proceed to run backorder processing directly from the application. Otherwise, you can schedule a background job for this task.

  3. Schedule BOP Run : Go to the Schedule BOP Run Fiori Application and select ATP: Backorder Processing Default Template. Maintain the scheduling details based on your business requirements. In the subsequent step, choose the variant created in the previous step and execute the background job. Upon completion, the system reallocates the confirmed quantities from low-priority customer sales orders to high-priority customer sales orders.

Note : This is a sample approach for performing backorder processing. You can define your own conditions when configuring the BOP segment

Please Let me Know If you have any concern.

Best Regards

Vivek Savant

 What am I doing wrong to not get any data back? Are there any restrictions? How can I troubleshoot this?

Thanks

entry.title: Anticipo Proveedor con OC de Activo Fijo
entry.content:
Hola,

Estoy tratando de crear un anticipo a un proveedor que tiene una orden de compra con imputacion de anticipo. el activo se ha creado como maquinaria en curso y la orden de compra se asocio este activo fijo creado, hasta ahi no problema, pero cuando quiero crear el anticipo me salta el siguiente error y no puedo continuar:

Diagnóstico

El número del activo fijo imputado   en el documento de compras

  • difiere del número de activo fijo introducido manualmente o
  • no puede copiarse, ya que el campo "Número de activo fijo" se ha suprimido para la cuenta en cuestión.

Actividades en el sistema

En el primer caso aparece una advertencia indicando diferentes imputaciones, en el segundo caso aparece un mensaje de error, ya que el número de activo fijo se debe tomar del pedido, pero está suprimido el campo correspondiente.

Procedimiento

En el caso de una advertencia se debe verificar cuál es el número del activo fijo para este anticipo.

En el caso de una advertencia se debe seleccionar otro indicador de operación en cuenta de mayor especial para anticipos, para que esté permitido la imputación de un número de activo fijo en la cuenta de mayor o se debe modificar o borrar el número del documento de compras indicado. Sólo están permitidos los documentos de compras que no se han imputado en activo fijo.

Nº mensaje: F5378

Si reviso la determinacion de cuenta y la GL asociada para el movimiento de anticipo tengo la cuenta:

 

Como puedo solucionar el error?

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Asset Management Sourcing and Procurement - Purchase Contract Management #downpayment
